principal_verdict: FAIL — SPATIAL MEMORY DOES NOT SURVIVE TURNOVER
one_line: h2 replicates as a prospectively-decodable, frame-independent coordinate, but it lives in the memory VALUE-DISPERSION (not a geometric pattern) and COLLAPSES under modest constituent turnover (gone by M~0.64) while h1 persists intact — so h2 is not a turnover-surviving organizational memory. Positive-claim transplant/causal battery not run (stopping rule).
hypothesis_outcome: H1 (organizational spatial memory) FALSIFIED by turnover; H4 (lab-frame artefact) REJECTED (frame-free decode robust; lab-frame edge collapses under translation); result ~ Outcome B (material/maintenance-bound) at the storage level. h1 remains a genuine 1-D turnover-surviving organizational memory.

decisions.reproduction_readiness: REPRODUCTION NOT READY (h2 not turnover-surviving; P4 fails)
decisions.genome_relevance: GENOME NOT REQUIRED (a non-persistent second coordinate does not justify a heritable code)
decisions.sealed_assets: HMC PROSPECTIVE SPLIT sealed; SC-PILOT-CAUSAL-FINGERPRINT blocked; EXP-SC-01 blocked — untouched

next_experiment: Not a new memory component or readout. The finding is that h1 (cumulative mean) is a turnover-surviving 1-D organizational memory while a second coordinate (dispersion) is destroyed by the memory's own self-maintenance (templating eta_t + diffusion D_m) + material renewal. If a second persistent coordinate is wanted, the honest next step is a WRITING/maintenance study of whether any minimal, viability-preserving change protects a second coordinate from homogenization across turnover — before any transplant/causal or reproduction work.
